# fhir

> A Rust implementation of the HL7 FHIR® Release 5 (R5) data model, plus a
> spec-driven code generator that produces it from the official FHIR
> specification JSON. Every resource and datatype is a `serde`-serializable Rust
> type that round-trips to and from canonical FHIR JSON. FHIR (Fast Healthcare
> Interoperability Resources) is the HL7 standard for exchanging electronic
> health records.

The model is comprehensive: 21 primitive datatypes, 50 complex datatypes,
158 resources, 400+ code-system enums, and a recursive validation layer. The
crate is imported as `fhir` (for example `use fhir::r5::resources::Patient;`).

## Crate layout

The public API lives under the `r5` module (FHIR Release 5):

- `r5::resources` — the 158 R5 resources as structs (Patient, Observation,
  Encounter, …), plus the polymorphic `Resource` enum tagged by `resourceType`
  for reading a resource of unknown type.
- `r5::types` — the ~50 complex datatypes (Period, HumanName, CodeableConcept,
  …) and the 21 primitive newtypes (Code, Id, DateTime, Boolean, Integer64, …).
  Primitives serialize transparently to their underlying JSON value.
- `r5::codes` — FHIR `CodeSystem`s as type-safe enums (for example
  `AdministrativeGender::Female` serializes to `"female"`).
- `r5::validate` — the `Validate` trait and `#[derive(Validate)]`, reporting
  pathed `ValidationIssue`s and checking primitive format constraints
  recursively.
- `r5::meta` — per-element metadata extracted from the specification
  (cardinality, coded-value bindings, `value[x]` choice types, reference target
  profiles, summary membership), queryable by FHIR path.
- `r5::choice` — support types for `value[x]` choice enums (`Primitive<T>`).
- `r5::coded` — the `Coded<E>` wrapper typing `required`-binding fields as their
  `codes` enum with an `Unknown(String)` fallback for wire compatibility.
- `r5::extension_ext` — the `ExtensionExt`/`ModifierExtensionExt` traits:
  `extension(url)`, `extensions(url)`, `set_extension`, `add_extension`.
- `r5::builder` — `BuilderError` for the generated `Type::builder()` builders.
- `r5::bundle_util` — `Bundle` iteration (`iter_resources`, `resources::<T>`),
  `next_link` paging, and the transaction/batch builder.
- `r5::summary` — `to_summary_value` (the FHIR `_summary=true` view via the
  `is_summary` metadata).
- `client` (feature `client`) — an async FHIR REST `Client` (reqwest/tokio):
  read/vread/create/update/delete/search/capabilities; errors carry `OperationOutcome`.
- `r5::xml` (feature `xml`) — FHIR XML `to_xml`/`from_xml` via a meta-driven
  serde_json::Value bridge.

The crate root also exposes `prelude` (`use fhir::prelude::*;`) re-exporting the
most-used resources, datatypes, `Validate`, `Coded`, and `ExtensionExt`.
- `r5::temporal` — parsing and precision-aware comparison for the date/time
  primitives (`Date::parse_parts`, `DateParts`, FHIR precision ordering).
- `r5::parse` — the spec-driven code generator that reads the FHIR specification
  JSON in `DEFINITIONS_DIR` and emits the Rust model.
- `r5::abstract_types` — base/abstract FHIR interfaces used by the generator.

Generator-internal support modules (not part of the consumer API):
`r5::properties`, `r5::resource`, `r5::todo`.

The crate root also exposes the top-level modules `r5` and `util`, the
`DEFINITIONS_DIR` path constant, and the `SourceCodeString` type alias.

## Key types

- `fhir::r5::resources::Resource` — polymorphic, `resourceType`-tagged enum.
- `fhir::r5::resources::Patient`, `Observation`, `Bundle`, … — resource structs.
- `fhir::r5::types::{Code, Id, DateTime, Boolean, Integer64}` — primitive newtypes.
- `fhir::r5::types::{HumanName, CodeableConcept, Period}` — complex datatypes.
- `fhir::r5::validate::{Validate, ValidationIssue}` — validation.

## Cardinality mapping

FHIR element cardinality maps to Rust types: `0..1` → `Option<T>`,
`1..1` → `T`, `0..*` → `Option<Vec<T>>`, `1..*` → `Vec<T>`. A `value[x]` choice
element is one generated enum per element (a variant per allowed type), and a
`required`-binding coded field is its `codes` enum wrapped in `Coded<E>`.

## Examples

Runnable programs in the `examples/` directory (`cargo run --example <name>`):

- `build_patient` — construct a Patient and serialize to canonical FHIR JSON.
- `validate_resource` — validate a resource and inspect `ValidationIssue`s.
- `read_bundle` — read a Bundle and dispatch on each entry's resource type.
- `code_systems` — use `CodeSystem` enums as type-safe coded values.

## Documentation

- `README.md` — overview and quick start.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — build/test commands and the code-generator workflow.
- `AGENTS.md` — guidance for AI coding agents and contributors.
- `spec/` — the living specifications (the source of truth).
- `CHANGELOG.md` — release history.
